name: Reverse Curl
resistance_profile: Pronated-grip curl emphasizing the brachioradialis and forearm extensors.
execution_steps: Stand holding a barbell with an overhand (pronated) grip, hands about shoulder-width, arms hanging straight.
Pin your elbows to your sides and keep your wrists firm and straight.
Curl the bar up by bending only at the elbows, without swinging.
Squeeze at the top, then lower under control to full arm extension.
good_when: You want to build the brachioradialis and forearm extensors along with the elbow flexors.
You want thicker-looking forearms and upper-arm balance.
You want a curl variation that also challenges grip.
bad_when: Your priority is peak biceps development, where a supinated curl loads them more.
The pronated grip causes wrist pain, where an EZ-bar or hammer curl is kinder.
You need to move heavy loads, since the weaker pronated grip limits the weight.
